holdings.name: State Bank of India
holdings.description: India's largest bank by deposits and assets — commanding ~22,000 branches and 60,000+ ATMs. Post-NPA-cleanup ROE is recovering to 15%+. YONO super app (70M+ users). Key PSU credit off-taker for government schemes — PM Housing, Kisan Credit and Jan Dhan.

holdings.name: Bank of Baroda
holdings.description: India's 2nd largest PSU bank. 109+ international offices (largest PSU bank international footprint). NIM recovery driven by CASA improvement and better pricing discipline post-Vijaya and Dena Bank mergers. ROE 16%+ for FY25.

holdings.name: Canara Bank
holdings.description: India's 3rd largest PSU bank. Strong in South India. Syndicate Bank merger has been well-integrated; cost synergies flowing through. NPA ratios below pre-pandemic levels. Key beneficiary of MSME and agriculture credit growth.

holdings.name: Punjab National Bank
holdings.description: India's 4th largest PSU bank. Post-fraud recovery complete — GNPA from 18% peak down to 5%. United Bank and Oriental Bank merger digested. North India distribution advantage for MSME and retail credit.

holdings.name: Union Bank of India
holdings.description: 3-way merger (Union + Andhra + Corporate Bank) created scale advantages. GNPA declining consistently. SME and MUDRA lending growing. Strong Western India rural customer base via original Andhra Bank presence.

holdings.name: Bank of India
holdings.description: India's 2nd oldest public sector bank. Global presence (22 countries). Trade finance and correspondent banking heritage. NPA resolution in large accounts gathering pace; provisions release driving earnings upgrades.
